questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The figure shows a block of mass $M=2 m$ having a spherical smooth cavity of radius $R$ placed on a smooth horizontal surface. There is a small ball of mass $m$ moving at an instant vertically downward with a velocity v with respect to the block. At this instant :
options: The normal reaction on the ball by the block is $\frac{\mathrm{mv}^2}{\mathrm{R}}$
The normal reaction on the ball by the block is $\frac{2}{3} \frac{m v^2}{R}$
The acceleration of the block with respect to the ground is $\frac{v^2}{3 R}$
The acceleration of the block with respect to the ground is $\frac{v^2}{2 R}$
questions.solution:
Let the normal force between the block and the ball be $N$.
For the block, from Newton's II ${ }^{\text {nd }}$ law, we have $\mathrm{N}=\mathrm{Ma}=2 \mathrm{ma}$ For ball (with respect to the block), from Newton's II ${ }^{\text {nd }}$ law, we have $N+m a=\frac{m v^2}{R}$ Solve the two equations.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A small rubber eraser is placed at one edge of a quarter-circle-shaped track of radius $R$ that lies in a vertical plane and has its axis of symmetry vertical (see figure); it is then released. The coefficient of friction between the eraser and the surface of the track is $\mu=0.6$.
options: If the particle slides from $A$ to $B$ work done by frictional force will be $\frac{0.6 \mathrm{mgR}}{\sqrt{2}}$.
If the particle slides from $A$ to $B$ work done by frictional force will be greater than $\frac{0.6 \mathrm{mg} \pi R}{4 \sqrt{2}}$.
The particle will never be able to go from $A$ to $B$.
If $\mu$ is $\sqrt{ } 2$ the particle will not begin to slide.
questions.solution:
Underestimate the work done against friction, and compare it with the initial gravitational potential energy of the eraser.
First of all, we investigate whether the rubber eraser will start moving at all. It will do so provided that $\mathrm{mg} \sin \alpha\gt\mu \mathrm{mg} \cos \alpha$, i.e. $\mu \lt \tan \alpha=\tan 45^{\circ}=1$. This is clearly the case, since $\mu=0.6$. So, the eraser will start moving. The trouble is that the determination of how the normal force acting on the eraser varies with position is difficult. A calculation of the work done against friction can be carried out, to any given degree of accuracy, only by using a computer. The trouble is that the determination of how the normal force acting on the eraser varies with position is difficult. However, it is certain that the frictional force is always larger than its initial value of $\mu \mathrm{mg} \cos \alpha=$ $\mu \mathrm{mg} \cos 45^{\circ}$. This is because, after the initial release, the angle with the horizontal made by the slope on which the eraser moves decreases, and, in addition, the track has to provide a centripetal force for the moving eraser. The path to reach the lowest point of the track would be one-eighth of a circle, with a length of Rm/4. The work done against friction can now be underestimated by taking the normal force as if it always had its initial value:
$\left|W_f\right|\gt\left|W_{\text {und }}\right|=\frac{\mu m g}{\sqrt{2}} \frac{\pi R}{4} \approx 0.333 m g R$
The gravitational potential energy difference between the initial position and the bottom of the track is $\Delta \mathrm{E}_{\mathrm{p}}=\operatorname{mgR}(1-\cos \alpha)=\operatorname{mgR}\left(1-\cos 45^{\circ}\right) \approx 0.293 \mathrm{mgR}$.
It can be seen that $\left|W_t\right|\gt\Delta E p$, i.e. the work to be done against friction is clearly larger than what can be provided by the gravitational potential energy. So, the rubber eraser cannot reach the very lowest part of the track.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A long thin uniform rod lies flat on the table as shown. One end of the rod is slowly pulled up by a force that remains perpendicular to the rod at all times. The rod is to be brought to the vertical position without any slipping of the bottom end? The coefficient of static friction between rod and ground can be:
questions.solution: Since the rod is raised slowly (quasistatically), the entire system remains in equilibrium at any moment in time. Thus, the torque about the point of rotation of the rod is zero about any axis, and the net external forces are $0 . \mathrm{F}_{\mathrm{fric}}=\mathrm{F} \sin \theta$ $\tau=-F I+\frac{m g l \cos \theta}{2}=0 \Rightarrow F=\frac{m g \cos \theta}{2}$ $N=m g-F \cos \theta$ The $\mathrm{F} \sin \theta$ force causes the rod's bottom end to slip in one direction. The friction force counters this force to stop the slipping when $F_{\text {fric }}=\mu_s N \geq F \sin \theta$. Hence, $\mu_{\mathrm{s}} \geq \frac{\mathrm{F} \sin \theta}{\mathrm{mg}-\mathrm{F} \cos \theta}=\frac{\sin \theta \cos \theta}{2-\cos ^2 \theta}$ Maximum of $\mu \mathrm{s}$ can be found by setting its derivative with respect to theta equal to zero: $\frac{d \mu}{d \theta}=\frac{\left(2-\cos ^2 \theta\right)\left(2 \cos ^2 \theta-1\right)-2 \cos ^2 \theta\left(1-\cos ^2 \theta\right)}{\left(2-\cos ^2 \theta\right)^2}=0 \text { This gives } \sin \theta=\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}} \text { and } \mu_{\min }=\frac{\sqrt{2}}{4}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Two 30 kg blocks rest on a massless belt which passes over a fixed pulley and is attached to a 40 kg block. If coefficient of friction between the belt and the table as well as between the belt and the blocks $B$ and $C$ is $\mu$ and the system is released from rest from the position shown, the speed with which the block B falls off the belt is/are: (Take $\mathrm{g}=10 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}^2$)
options: $2 \sqrt{2} \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$ if $\mu=0.2$
$\sqrt{2} \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$ if $\mu=0.2$
$2 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$ if $\mu=0.5$
$2.5 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$ if $\mu=0.5$
questions.solution:
Maximum possible acceleration of block will be $=\mu \mathrm{g}$. (i) When $\mu=0.2$ maximum acceleration possible so that block does not slip is $2 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}^2$ Now acceleration of system assuming blocks don't slip is
$\mathrm{a}=\frac{40 \mathrm{~g}-2 \mu \mathrm{mg}}{100}=2.8 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}^2 \quad \because \mu=0.2$ as $2.8\gt2$. Hence the assumption is wrong and then blocks slip on the belt. $\therefore \quad$ Maximum force applicable on block B is $\mu \mathrm{mg}$ $\therefore$ acc. of block $=2 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}^2$ Hence velocity of $B$ when it strikes pulley is $\mathrm{V}^2=0^2+(2 \times 2 \times 2)$ $V^2=8 \quad \therefore \quad V=2 \sqrt{2} \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$ Hence (A). (ii) When $\mu=0.5$ maximum acceleration possible so that block does not slip is $5 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}^2$ Now acceleration of system, assuming blocks don't slip is
$a=\frac{40 \mathrm{~g}-2 \mu \mathrm{mg}}{100}=1 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}^2 ; \mu=0.5$ as $1 \lt 5$ hence, blocks do not slip and acceleration of block B is $1 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}^2$ $\therefore \quad$ Hence velocity of B when it strikes pulley is $\mathrm{V}^2=0^2+(2 \times 1 \times 2)$ $\begin{aligned} & V^2=4 \\ \therefore \quad & V=2 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s} \end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The linear mass density of a rod of length $\ell$ depends on the distance x from upper end as $\lambda=\lambda_0 \mathrm{e}^{2 \mathrm{x}}$. Rod is moved up by applying a force F. Find the correct statement.
options: The minimum force required to move the rod up is $\frac{\lambda_0}{2}\left(e^{2 \ell}-1\right) g$.
The tension at the mid point of the rod is $\mathrm{F} / 2$
The acceleration of the rod is $g$ upward if $F=\lambda_{\mathrm{d}}\left(e^{2 t}-1\right) g$
The tension at the mid-point will be less if rod moves on a smooth horizontal surface.
questions.solution:
For $(\mathrm{A}): F_{\min }=m g=\left(\int d m\right) g=\int(\lambda d \ell) g=\int_0^{\ell}\left(\lambda_0 e^{2 x} d x\right) g=\frac{\lambda_0}{2}\left(e^{2 \ell}-1\right) g$
For (B) : Lower half is heavier so, $T\gt\frac{F}{2}$
For (C) : $F-m g=m a \Rightarrow F=m(g+a)=\lambda_0\left(e^{2 \ell}-1\right) g$ and $a=g$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A rope $A B$ of linear mass density $\lambda$ is placed on a quarter vertical fixed disc of radius R as shown in the figure. The surface between the disc and rope is rough such that the rope is just in equilibrium. Gravitational acceleration is g . Choose the correct option(s).
options: Coefficient of static friction between rope and disc is $\mu=1$.
Coefficient of static friction between rope and disc is $\mu=\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}$.
Maximum tension in the rope is at the top most point A of the rope.
Maximum tension in the rope is $\lambda \operatorname{Rg}(\sqrt{2}-1)$.
questions.solution: For equilibrium $\begin{aligned} & \lambda \operatorname{Rg} \int_0^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \cos \theta d \theta=\mu \lambda \operatorname{Rg} \int_0^{\frac{\pi}{2}} \sin \theta d \theta \\ & \therefore \mu=1 \end{aligned}$ At the position of maximum tension in the rope $\lambda R d \theta g \cos \theta=\mu(\lambda R d \theta g \sin \theta)$ $\therefore \theta=45^{\circ}$ At any $\theta$, $d T=\lambda R d \theta g \cos \theta-\mu \lambda R d \theta g \sin \theta$ $\begin{aligned} & \int_0^{T_{\max }} d T=\lambda \operatorname{Rg} \int_0^{\frac{\pi}{4}}(\cos \theta-\sin \theta) d \theta \\ & T_{\max }=\lambda \operatorname{Rg}[\sin \theta+\cos \theta]_0^{\frac{\pi}{4}}=\lambda \operatorname{Rg}\left[\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}+\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}-1\right]=\lambda \operatorname{Rg}(\sqrt{2}-1) \end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A disc of radius a and mass $m$ rests between two light rods, which are smoothly hinged together at point $O$ with their ends $A$ and $B$ resting on a smooth table and are maintained in equilibrium by string AB as shown in the figure. Choose the correct option (s).
options: The magnitude of normal force acting on the rod at point A is $\frac{\mathrm{mg}}{2}$.
The magnitude of normal force acting on the rod at point A is $\frac{\mathrm{mg}}{2 \cos \alpha}$.
The tension in the string $A B$ is $\frac{\mathrm{mg}}{2}\left[\cot \alpha+\frac{\mathrm{a}}{\mathrm{c}} \sec ^2 \alpha\right]$
The tension in the string AB is $\frac{\mathrm{mg}}{2}\left[\tan \alpha+\frac{\mathrm{a}}{\mathrm{c}} \operatorname{cosec}^2 \alpha\right]$
questions.solution: For unit length of wire, $\mathrm{F}_{\mathrm{e}}=\lambda \mathrm{E} \Rightarrow$ electric force $=\lambda \frac{2 \lambda}{4 \pi \varepsilon_0 \ell}=\frac{\lambda^2}{2 \pi \varepsilon_0 \ell}$ $\lambda \Rightarrow$ excess charge per unit length of wire $\mathrm{F}_{\mathrm{m}} \Rightarrow \frac{\mu_0 \mathrm{I}^2}{2 \pi t} \Rightarrow \text { magnetic force }$ According to given question $\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{F}_{\mathrm{c}}=\mathrm{F}_{\mathrm{m}} \\ & \frac{\lambda^2}{2 \pi \varepsilon_0 \ell}=\frac{\mu_0 \mathrm{I}^2}{2 \pi \ell} \\ & \Rightarrow \quad\left(\frac{1}{\lambda}\right)^2=\frac{1}{\varepsilon_0 \mu_0} \\ & \Rightarrow \quad\left(\frac{\ell \mathrm{n} 20}{\pi \varepsilon_0 \mathrm{~V}} \times \frac{\mathrm{V}}{\mathrm{R}}\right)^2=\frac{1}{\mu_0 \varepsilon_0} \\ & \Rightarrow \quad \mathrm{R}=\sqrt{\frac{\mu_0}{\varepsilon_0}}\left(\frac{\ell \ln 20}{\pi}\right)=360 \Omega \end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A uniform cube of mass 4 kg and side 30 cm is placed on a frictionless horizontal surface. A vertical force F is applied to the edge as shown in the figure. Choose the most appropriate option(s). (take $\left.\mathrm{g}=10 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}^2\right)$
options: Cube will move in the upward direction if $\mathrm{F}\gt40 \mathrm{~N}$
Cube will not perform any type of translation or rotation motion if $10 \mathrm{~N} \lt \mathrm{F} \lt 20 \mathrm{~N}$
cube will begin to perform rotational motion if $20 \mathrm{~N} \lt \mathrm{F} \lt 40 \mathrm{~N}$
Normal reaction applied by the surface shifts to a distance 5 cm from centre C of the cube if $\mathrm{F}=10 \mathrm{~N}$
questions.solution: The correct answer is option A,B,C,D. Momentum of segment of mass dm and length $\mathrm{d} \ell$ $\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{d} \overrightarrow{\mathrm{p}}=\mathrm{dmu}(\cos \theta \hat{\mathbf{i}}+\sin \theta \hat{\mathrm{j}}) \\ & =\mathrm{u} \mathrm{\lambda}[\mathrm{~d} \ell \cos \theta \hat{\mathrm{i}}+\mathrm{d} \ell \sin \theta \hat{\mathrm{j}}] \\ & \overrightarrow{\mathrm{P}}=\mu \lambda\left[\int \mathrm{dx} \hat{\mathrm{i}}+\int \mathrm{dy} \hat{\mathrm{j}}\right] \\ & \overrightarrow{\mathrm{p}}=\mathrm{u} \lambda[\mathrm{~d} \hat{\mathrm{i}}+\mathrm{h} \hat{\mathrm{j}}] \\ & \mathrm{P}=\mathrm{u} \lambda \sqrt{\mathrm{~d}^2+\mathrm{h}^2} \\ & =1 \times 1 \sqrt{3^2+4^2}=5 \mathrm{~kg}-\mathrm{m} / \mathrm{s} \end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: At $\mathrm{t}=0$, a force $\mathrm{F}=\mathrm{at}^2$ is applied to a small body of mass $m$ at an angle $\alpha$ resting on a smooth horizontal plane
options: velocity of the body at the moment it brakes off the plane is
the distance travelled by the body before breaking off the plane is $\frac{\mathrm{mg}^2}{12 \mathrm{a} \sin \alpha \tan \alpha}$.
Its acceleration at the time of breaking off the plane is $\mathrm{g} \cot \alpha$.
Time at which it breaks off the plane is $\sqrt{\frac{\mathrm{mg}}{\mathrm{a} \sin \alpha}}$.
questions.solution:
For breaking off the plane:
$\begin{aligned}
& \mathrm{F} \sin \alpha=\mathrm{mg} \\
& \Rightarrow \mathrm{a} \mathrm{t}_0^2 \sin \alpha=\mathrm{mg} \\
& \Rightarrow \mathrm{t}_0=\sqrt{\frac{\mathrm{mg}}{\mathrm{a} \sin \alpha}} .
\end{aligned}$

Speed at time of breaking off.
$\begin{aligned}
& \mathrm{v}=\int_0^{\mathrm{b}} \frac{\mathrm{at}^2 \cos \alpha}{\mathrm{~m}} \mathrm{dt}=\frac{\mathrm{at} \mathrm{t}_0^2 \cos \alpha}{3 \mathrm{~m}} \\
& =\frac{\mathrm{a} \cos \alpha}{3 \mathrm{~m}} \cdot \frac{\mathrm{mg}}{\mathrm{a} \sin \alpha} \sqrt{\frac{\mathrm{mg}}{\mathrm{a} \sin \alpha}} \\
& =\sqrt{\frac{\mathrm{mg}^3}{9 \tan ^2 \alpha \sin \alpha}} \\
& \mathrm{a}=\frac{\mathrm{F} \cos \alpha}{\mathrm{~m}}=\frac{\mathrm{at}_0^2 \cos \alpha}{\mathrm{~m}}=\frac{\mathrm{amg}}{\mathrm{a} \sin \alpha} \cdot \frac{\cos \alpha}{\mathrm{~m}} \\
& =\mathrm{g} \cot \alpha \text {. } \\
& s=\int \mathrm{vdt}=\int_0^b \frac{\mathrm{t}^3}{3 \mathrm{~m}} \cos \alpha \mathrm{dt} \\
& =\frac{a}{12 m} t_0^4=\frac{a}{12 m} \cdot \frac{m^2 g^2 \cos \alpha}{a^2 \sin ^2 \alpha} \\
& =\frac{\mathrm{mg}^2}{12 \mathrm{a} \tan \alpha \sin \alpha} \text {. }
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The figure shows a block of mass $m$ placed on a smooth wedge of mass $M$. Calculate the value of $M^{\prime}$ and tension in the string, so that the block of mass $m$ will move vertically downward with acceleration $10 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}^2$. (Take $\mathrm{g}=10 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}^2$ )
options: the value of $M^{\prime}$ is $\frac{M \cot \theta}{1-\cot \theta}$
the value of $M^{\prime} \frac{M \tan \theta}{1-\tan \theta}$
the value of tension in the string is $\frac{\mathrm{Mg}}{\tan \theta}$
the value of tension is $\frac{\mathrm{Mg}}{\cot \theta}$
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & M^{\prime} g-T=M^{\prime} a ..(i)\\ & T=M a ..(ii)\\ & M^{\prime} g=a\left(M+M^{\prime}\right) \\ & a=\frac{M^{\prime} g}{\left(M+M^{\prime}\right)} \\ & m a \sin \theta=m g \cos \theta \\ & a=g \cot \theta \\ & g \cot \theta=\frac{M^{\prime} g}{\left(M+M^{\prime}\right)} \\ & \cot \theta M+\cot \theta M^{\prime}=M^{\prime} \\ & M^{\prime}=\frac{M \cot \theta}{(1-\cot \theta)} \\ & T=M a \\ & =M \cdot g \cot \theta \\ & T=\frac{M g}{\tan \theta}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: In the figure small block is kept on $m$ then
options: the acceleration of m w.r.t. ground is $\frac{\mathrm{F}}{\mathrm{m}}$
the acceleration of $m$ w.r.t. ground is zero
the time taken by $m$ to separate from $M$ is $\sqrt{\frac{2 \ell m}{F}}$
the time taken by $m$ to separate from $M$ is $\sqrt{\frac{2 C M}{F}}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Figure shows three blocks on a rough surface under influence of a force P of same magnitude in all the three cases. Coefficient of friction is same between each block and ground. What possible relation holds between magnitudes of normal reaction and friction forces. [Assume that blocks do not overturn about edge, $\mathrm{f}_A, \mathrm{f}_{\mathrm{B}} \& \mathrm{f}_{\mathrm{C}}$ are frictional forces $\& \mathrm{~N}_{\mathrm{A}}, \mathrm{N}_{\mathrm{B}} \& \mathrm{~N}_{\mathrm{C}}$ are reactions]
options: $\mathrm{N}_{\mathrm{A}}\gt\mathrm{N}_{\mathrm{C}}\gt\mathrm{N}_{\mathrm{B}}$
$\mathrm{f}_{\mathrm{A}}\gt\mathrm{f}_{\mathrm{C}}\gt\mathrm{f}_{\mathrm{B}}$
$\mathrm{f}_{\mathrm{C}}\gt\mathrm{f}_{\mathrm{A}}=\mathrm{f}_{\mathrm{B}}$
$\mathrm{N}_{\mathrm{C}}\gt\mathrm{N}_{\mathrm{A}}=\mathrm{N}_{\mathrm{B}}$
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{N}=\mathrm{mg}+\mathrm{P} \sin \theta \\ & \mathrm{f}_{\max }=\mu \mathrm{N} \\ & \text { Friction may be static }=P \cos \theta\end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned} & N=m g-P \sin \theta \\ & f_{\max }=\mu \mathrm{N} \\ & \text { Friction may be static }=P \cos \theta\end{aligned}$
$$ \begin{aligned} & \mathrm{N}=\mathrm{mg} \\ & \mathrm{f}_{\max }=\mu \mathrm{N} \end{aligned} $$ Friction may be static
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A point object of mass $m$ is slipping down on a smooth hemispherical body of mass $M$ and radius $R$. The point object is tied to a wall by an ideal string as shown. At a certain instant shown in figure, speed of the hemisphere is $v$ and its acceleration is $a$. Then speed $v_p$ and acceleration $a_p$ of the point object is. (Assume all the surfaces in contact are frictionless).
options: $v_p=v \sin 60^{\circ}$
$V_P=V$
$a_p=a$
$a_p=\sqrt{\left(\frac{a \sqrt{3}}{2}+\frac{v^2}{R}\right)^2+\left(\frac{a}{2}\right)^2}$
questions.solution:
$\mathrm{x}+\mathrm{R} \theta=$ constant $\frac{d x}{d t}+\frac{R d \theta}{d t}=0$ $\therefore \omega=\frac{d \theta}{d t}=\frac{v}{R}$
$\begin{aligned} & \alpha=\mathrm{d} \omega / \mathrm{dt}=\mathrm{a} / \mathrm{R} \\ & \text { At } \theta=60^{\circ},\end{aligned}$ $\therefore \quad v_p=v$ and $a_p=\sqrt{\left(\frac{a \sqrt{3}}{2}+\frac{v^2}{R}\right)^2+\left(\frac{a}{2}\right)^2}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A block of mass $m(=0.1 \mathrm{kg})$ is hanging over a frictionless light fixed pulley by an inextensible string of negligible mass. The other end of the string is pulled by a constant force $F$ in the vertically downward direction. The linear momentum of the block increases by $2 \mathrm{kgms}^{-1}$ in $1 \mathrm{s}$ after the block starts from rest. Then, (given $g=10 \mathrm{ms}^{-2}$ )
options: The tension in the string is $F$
The tension in the string is $3 \mathrm{N}$
The work done by the tension on the block is $20 \mathrm{J}$ during this 1 s
The work done against the force of gravity is $10 \mathrm{J}$
questions.solution:
The free body diagram
$\Rightarrow \quad F=2+m g=3 N$
also, $a=\frac{\text { unbalanced force }}{\text { mass }}=\frac{2}{0.1}=20 \mathrm{m} / \mathrm{s}^{2}$
$S=\frac{1}{2} a t^{2}=\frac{1}{2} \times 20 \times 1=10 \mathrm{m}$
Honce, work done by tension
$$
=F \times 10=3 \times 10=30 \mathrm{J}
$$
So, the work done against gravity
$$
=m g \times S=1 \times 10=10 \mathrm{J}
$$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
